data.title: 'I haven't filed taxes in 20 years': I'm 55 and about to get laid off. I've $1 million in crypto. Am I in big trouble?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'I have $800,000 split across two 401(k) plans and a $69,000 lump-sum pension'

"I believe Social Security will be insolvent in 15 years. I would welcome the extra check, but I'm not counting on it." (Photo subject is a model.)

Dear Quentin,

I'm 55 and expect to get laid off from my $170,000 job. I have $800,000 split across two 401(k) plans and a $69,000 lump-sum pension. My wife and I each have $150,000 in Roth IRAs, $400,000 in real-estate equity (with a mortgage of less than $50,000) and about $1 million in crypto (up 88% over 12 months and climbing daily).

My wife is the sole heir to her childhood home, inching towards $1 million. I took out a $1 million 20-year term-life policy on myself, but I will likely outlive it with only four years left. We have no college debt, credit cards, kids or car payments. We spend about $60,000 yearly (including leisure travel and $12,000 on the mortgage).

I expect to use "rule of 55" withdrawals to pay off the mortgage and sell small amounts of crypto to carry us to age 59 1/2. I don't plan to claim Social Security until age 70, when my wife will likely inherit the house. I believe Social Security will be insolvent in 15 years. I would welcome the extra check, but I'm not counting on it.

Don't miss: Should I apply for a $70K-a-year job at 74 and risk losing my Section 8 housing allowance?

Selling crypto, paying off mortgage

Crypto has been good to me, but it will likely take a year or two to adjust to the new regulatory landscape. In the meantime, paying off the mortgage and selling a little crypto here and there is my plan to tread water for five years to reach 59 1/2. Can I retire early with these numbers? Or am I expecting too much from my crypto?

The elephant in the room is that I haven't filed taxes in 20 years. I've always had taxes withheld and I'm not hiding any undeclared or illicit income; I just haven't filed in forever. I only bought and held crypto starting 10 years ago with KYC-compliant purchases, so over 97% of my balance is unrealized gains - no unpaid taxes there.

I don't know what professional help I need to get right with the Internal Revenue Service and several states. A CPA and tax attorney? I feel like we have enough retirement savings. The future rental income will certainly help, and the crypto may grow into the millions. I just don't want to leave a mess for my wife if I die first.

Stumbling into the FIRE

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com. The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

Don't miss: I'm a senior who barely survives on $1,300 a month. No way could I live on $1,000.

Between your $869,000 in tax-deferred accounts and your $300,000 Roth, a 4% annual withdrawal rate would give you $48,000 a year.

Dear Stumbling,

You buried the lede.

You have organized all other aspects of your finances, so it's unfortunate that you dropped the ball with the IRS, the one creditor that never goes away. With that in mind, hire a CPA and immediately throw yourself at the IRS's mercy. That sounds dramatic, yes - but it sounds worse than the reality. I suspect anxiety over not filing has kept you from dealing with this issue.

Given that you were employed during this time, it's not as bad as it could be if you were self-employed and were living off the tax grid. "The IRS prefers that you file all back tax returns for years you have not yet filed," TurboTax says. "That said, the IRS usually only requires you to file the last six years of tax returns to be considered in good standing."

But you may have missed out on years of tax refunds. "The IRS can go back more than six years in certain instances," it adds. "Unfortunately, there is a limit on how far back you can file a tax return to claim tax refunds and tax credits. This IRS only allows you to claim refunds and tax credits within three years of the tax return's original due date."

Don't miss: My wife and I are in our 50s and have $11 million. We're not leaving it to our kids. Is that wrong?

'Rule of 55'

You're in good shape to retire early, even taking into consideration that crypto can be highly volatile. But even if your crypto portfolio fell by 50% over the next year (and there's no existing reason that it should), you'd still be able to live and maintain something close to your current lifestyle. You may, however, have to rein in your vacation expenses.

A reminder for some of our readers: The "rule of 55" is an IRS provision that allows you to take penalty-free withdrawals from your current employer's 401(k) or 403(b) if you leave your job in the year you turn 55 or later without incurring a 10% early-withdrawal 10% penalty. Of course, you'll still have to pay regular income tax on those withdrawals.

In order to keep up with your current level of spending, you can rely on 401(k) withdrawals under the "rule of 55" and take $40,000 to $50,000 a year in addition to $10,000 to $20,000 of crypto sales. Between your $869,000 in tax-deferred accounts and your $300,000 Roth, a 4% annual withdrawal rate would give you $48,000 a year.

Applying the "rule of 55" will be dependent on the conditions of your plan, specifically whether the administrator permits partial and early withdrawals. "Many plans do not offer partial withdrawals once you've left your job," Fidelity says. "This could mean you'll need to withdraw the entire account balance at once - potentially incurring taxes, even if the penalty is waived."

"If your workplace plan is a Roth 401(k), withdrawals will avoid penalties under the 'rule of 55,'" the company adds. "While the 'rule of 55' grants early access to funds, it's important to consider the long-term impact on your retirement savings. Withdrawing significant amounts could reduce your portfolio's growth potential and affect your future financial security."

Related: My wife and I are in our 50s and have $11 million. We're not leaving it to our kids. Is that wrong?

On the way to 65

Adding your Social Security benefits at age 70 and your wife's future inheritance, you have enough of a cushion to get you through the next 15 years. If your wife doesn't work, you will also have to pay for health insurance until you reach 65 and qualify for Medicare. Factor those costs into your plans.

You may also wish to roll over your former 401(k) into your existing employer's plan before your expected layoff, if you want access to more of your funds penalty-free using the aforementioned "rule of 55." This would allow you to leave your Roth IRA untouched for later so it can continue to grow tax-free.

As for your $1 million life-insurance policy, you don't have kids who will benefit from it and, while nobody knows what's around the corner, you will probably live a long life beyond the 20-year term, so you will have a decision to make about whether to continue paying the growing premiums. That also raises the topic of long-term care insurance.

Call that CPA today.

Related: 'The selling agent is long dead': My $250,000 term life-insurance policy costs $2,000 a month. I'm 80. Is it time to ditch it?

Check out the Moneyist private Facebook group, where we look for answers to life's thorniest money issues. Post your questions or weigh in on the latest Moneyist columns.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms.

More columns from Quentin Fottrell:

'I'm confused!' Why does President Trump want a rate cut so badly?

'I am a senior citizen': My car needs $3,500 for repairs, but only has a trade-in value of $6,000. Do I bother fixing it?

'I'm tired of corporate America': My wife and I have $1.65 million. I'm 61. Can I retire already?

-Quentin Fottrell

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  09-26-25 0932ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Amgen to Invest $650 Million in U.S. Production Expansion as Trump Hikes Tariffs
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller 




  Amgen said it would invest $650 million in expanding its U.S manufacturing network, a day after President Trump said he would impose a 100% tariff on pharmaceuticals produced by companies that are not building plants in the U.S. 



  Amgen said the investment would support production at the company's manufacturing facility in Juncos, Puerto Rico. The company said the expansion builds on recent investments in the U.S., including manufacturing expansions of $900 million in Ohio and $1 billion in North Carolina. 



  On Thursday, President Trump said he would impose a new 100% tariff on pharmaceuticals but that companies building out manufacturing in the U.S. would be exempt. 



  "Starting October 1st, 2025, we will be imposing a 100% Tariff on any branded or patented Pharmaceutical Product, unless a Company IS BUILDING their Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Plant in America," Trump posted on his social media site, Truth Social, on Thursday. 





  Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com. 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  September 26, 2025 09:33 ET (13:33 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 9 AM ET: Inflation rises again, PCE shows, but not enough to alarm the Fed | Stock ...
data.description:
Inflation rises again, PCE shows, but not enough to alarm the Fed

Prices aren't rising so fast as to postpone rate cuts

----

Stock Futures Edge Higher After Trump Unveils New Tariffs

Stock futures inched higher after President Trump unveiled new tariffs on drugs, trucks and furniture. Markets took the news in their stride.

----

Fed's Barkin upbeat on U.S. economic outlook despite rising inflation, slower job growth

"The consumer is frankly exhausted," Tom Barkin said Friday.

----

Trump to Impose New Tariffs on Pharma, Big Trucks

Levies on drugs will be imposed on companies that aren't building plants in the U.S.

----

Fed Independence Reaches Its Moment of Truth as Supreme Court Weighs Cook's Fate

The court is poised to decide whether President Trump can remove Lisa Cook from the Federal Reserve's board, which former officials see as threat to central bank independence.

----

Tokyo's Price Growth Steady But Rate Increase Still in Play

The pace of price growth in the Tokyo metropolitan area unexpectedly held steady in September, but upward pressure remains, keeping an interest-rate hike in play.

----

Pharma Stocks Vary Worldwide Following New Trump Tariffs

Big pharma stocks rose slightly in U.S. premarket trading, small and midsize companies in Europe were hit, and Asian pharma stocks fell across the board, leading the markets lower.

----

Democrats Dig In on Shutdown Stance After White House Threatens to Fire Workers

Republicans want a short-term deal to keep the government open, while Democratic leaders seek healthcare funds.

----

Europe's Right-Wing Populists Swerve Left on the Economy

The adoption of a high-spending, pro-welfare and protectionist agenda has been key in winning over blue-collar workers.

----

Demand for investment-grade bond ETFs surges as Oracle pushes up supply of new debt

Investors have piled back into exchange-traded funds focused on investment-grade corporate bonds, amid a surging supply of new debt in that part of the fixed-income market.

----

Bank of Mexico Makes 10th Straight Interest-Rate Cut

The board of governors voted 4-1 to lower the overnight interest-rate target to 7.50% from 7.75%.

----

Could the Fed skip a rate cut next month?

Economic data has been stronger than expected and several voting members of Federal Reserve's interest-rate committee have expressed caution about easing. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  September 26, 2025 09:15 ET (13:15 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: TrebleHook 25.3 Release: Turning Pursuit Chaos into Predictable Revenue
data.description:
TrebleHook 25.3 Release: Turning Pursuit Chaos into Predictable Revenue

TrebleHook 25.3 Release: Turning Pursuit Chaos into Predictable Revenue

PR Newswire

GREENVILLE, S.C., Sept. 26, 2025

GREENVILLE, S.C., Sept. 26, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- TrebleHook, the purpose-built CRM for AEC firms, today announced the release of Version 25.3, introducing new scheduling, billing, and marketing capabilities that help firms better forecast revenue and prove ROI. With this release, firms can see when work will hit, who it impacts, and which efforts are paying off—all without leaving TrebleHook.

Version 25.3 captures pursuit data, builds live project schedules, applies smart billing curves, and ties marketing programs, awards, and SF330 codes into one system of truth. These tools allow executives and marketers alike to turn fragmented data into confident planning and predictable growth.

Key Features in TrebleHook 25.3

Project Schedules Gantt – A new Gantt view displays the full portfolio of active projects, color-coded by stage and viewable by month, week, or year. Exports to PDF, PNG, or Excel make it easy to share workload forecasts and spot timing gaps across customers and divisions.

Project Billings (with Curves) – Professional or higher users can now split project values into monthly billings using one of 13 billing curves. Billings recalculate dynamically and can be visualized in dashboards, supporting cash planning, hiring, and backlog health.

Marketing Programs – Marketers can now manage campaigns, events, and tradeshows in TrebleHook and connect them directly to pursuits and contacts. Built-in filters and targeting tools make it easy to prove the ROI from marketing effort → pursuits → revenue.

Awards & Certifications – Track awards, licenses, and recognitions across employees, projects, and accounts, then instantly pull them into resumes and proposals to prove qualifications.

Project Descriptions & SF330 Codes – Create tailored project descriptions for different audiences and tag projects with official SF330 codes for faster, compliant resume creation in federal pursuits.

Education Tracking – Capture and report on employee education and degrees for quick inclusion in pursuit and resume materials.

About TrebleHook

TrebleHook is the CRM built for AEC firms to transform pursuit chaos into predictable revenue. By uniting people, projects, and pursuits into one system, TrebleHook empowers firms to streamline processes, forecast with confidence, and win more of the right work.

Learn more at www.treblehook.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/treblehook-25-3-release-turning-pursuit-chaos-into-predictable-revenue-302567886.html

SOURCE TrebleHook

data.title: Italian Regulator Fines Oil Companies $1.1 Billion in Price-Fixing Probe — Commodities Roundup
data.description:
MARKET MOVEMENTS:

--Brent crude oil is up 0.2% at $68.73 a barrel

--European benchmark gas is up 0.4% to 32.60 euros a megawatt-hour

--Gold futures are up 0.4% to $3,784.80 a troy ounce

--LME three-month copper futures are down 0.6% to $10,213 a metric ton 




  TOP STORY: 



  Italian Regulator Fines Oil Companies $1.1 Billion in Price-Fixing Probe 



  Italy's competition authority imposed more than $1.09 billion of fines on Eni and five other oil companies after closing a probe into allegations they set up a cartel to raise fuel prices. 



  The Italian Competition Authority on Friday closed its two-year investigation into the country's most important oil operators and said that some of them coordinated to set the value of the bio component factored into fuel prices. The regulator imposed fines totaling 936.7 million euros ($1.09 billion), it said. 



  Eni received the largest fine at 336 million euros, while ExxonMobil's Esso's totaled 129 million euros. The regulator levied 173 million euros on Q8, 164 million euros on Ip, 91 million euros on Tamoil, and 44 million euros on Saras. 





  OTHER STORIES: 



  Low-Cost Clean Energy Now Trumps Politics, Architect of Paris Accord Says 



  Wind, solar, and other forms of clean energy are slowly becoming more dominant the world over, former United Nations climate chief Christiana Figueres said. That is despite the U.S. president calling it a scam. 



  "The technologies--what we call electrotech--have taken off exponentially," Figueres, who was an executive of the U.N. Framework Convention on Climate Change and one of the key architects of the Paris Agreement, said. "Most clean technologies, whether it is in energy generation or in transport, are already cost competitive, or even out compete the technologies that would be fossil fuel based." 



  -- 



  Norway's Oil Fund to Invest $1.5 Billion in Brookfield Energy Transition Fund 



  Norges Bank Investment Management, the arm of Norway's central bank that manages the country's $2 trillion sovereign-wealth fund, agreed to invest $1.5 billion in Brookfield Asset Management's latest energy transition fund. 



  NBIM said since receiving a mandate to invest in unlisted renewable energy infrastructure in 2019, it has completed eight direct investments in European solar, onshore and offshore wind, and electricity transmission systems, as well as one indirect investment through a global renewable energy fund. 



  -- 



  EIG Accelerates Investing While Expanding LNG Operator MidOcean 



  Private fund manager EIG Global Energy Partners has been investing at a fast pace across the energy industry while making a particular effort to expand MidOcean Energy, its liquefied-natural-gas business, including a multibillion-dollar commitment to a Gulf Coast LNG project, its first in the U.S. 



  Washington-based EIG has made 51 deals with a total value of $36 billion during the past four years, according to Blair Thomas, chairman and chief executive. The firm invests in both the fossil-fuel and clean-energy sectors. 





  MARKET TALKS: 



  Silver, Platinum Extend Gains on Precious-Metals Rally -- Market Talk 



  1204 GMT - Silver prices continue to rally, with New York futures trading 2.4% higher to $44.94 a troy ounce in early afternoon trade after rising above $45 in the previous session. "The price of gold may have recently reached a new record high, but the price of silver has actually performed even more impressively," Thu Lan Nguyen, head of commodity strategy at Commerzbank, says. The precious metal has risen by nearly 56% this year, outperforming even gold, which has gained about 43%. "The record high price of gold is now deterring many and...alternatives are therefore being sought," she says. Meanwhile, platinum also rises, up 1.8% to $1,557.20 an ounce, its highest level since 2013. The white metal has surged 71% this year on expectations of another annual supply deficit. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com) 



  -- 



  Shares in European Steel Companies Rise on Reports of EU Tariffs on China -- Market Talk 



  1046 GMT - Shares in European steel companies rise on Friday after reports that the EU Commission will impose 25% to 50% tariffs on Chinese steel and related products. The tariffs come as President Trump closes off the U.S. market, leading China to divert large quantities of goods to Europe and pushing down prices, German newspaper Handelsblatt first reported Thursday. Shares in German steel producers Thyssenkrupp and Salzgitter rise 1.8% and 4.1%, respectively, while Austrian peer Voestalpine rises 2.5%. Arcelormittal and Helsinki-headquartered Outokumpu both rise 1.7%. In Spain, Acerinox rises 1.7%. "This goes to show the U.S. might not be the only winner from tariffs--others are deploying the same strategy with foreign trading partners to protect domestic producers," AJ Bell's Russ Mould says in a market comment. (nina.kienle@wsj.com) 



  -- 



  Palm Oil Closes Lower, Tracking Soybean Oil -- Market Talk 



  1014 GMT - Palm oil fell, tracking lower soybean oil prices, David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg, says. The narrowing price spread between the two competing oils likely weighed on market sentiment, Ng says. He sees support at 4,300 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,520 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for December fell 43 ringgit to 4,396 ringgit a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com) 



  -- 



  Crude Exports From Western Russia Hit 10-Month High This Month, Kpler Says 



  0917 GMT - Crude oil exports from Western Russia surged to a 10-month high this month driven by a sharp increase in Urals crude loadings amid intensifying Ukrainian drone strikes against key infrastructure, Kpler says. "Following these attacks, Russian crude oil runs are expected to slip to an annual low of below 5 million barrels a day in September," senior market analyst Yui Torikata says. "This forces producers to redirect crude that would have been processed domestically to the export market, with Kpler expecting seaborne crude oil exports to serve as a primary outlet for this displaced crude in 4Q." Exports have averaged around 2.5 million barrels a day in September, a level not reached since October, and are projected to stay at about 2.25 million barrels a day if all scheduled loadings are completed by the end of the month, according to the data provider. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com) 



  -- 



  Copper Softens But Stays on Track for Weekly Gain -- Market Talk 



  0848 GMT - Copper prices trade lower, with futures on the London Metal Exchange falling 0.5% to $10,226 a metric ton, but remain on track for a weekly gain of more than 2%. The industrial metal retreated on Thursday after surging nearly 4% on news that U.S. miner Freeport-McMoRan declared force majeure on contracted supplies from its Grasberg mine in Indonesia, fueling concerns about tightening global supplies. Meanwhile, Peru's Antamina mine is set to increase copper production by around 20% to 450,000 in 2026 and aims at around 400,000 annually in the following years, Bloomberg reported. "If achieved, Antamina could overtake Freeport-McMoran's Cerro Verde and MMG's Las Bambas, positioning itself as one of the country's top copper producers," ING analysts say. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com) 



  -- 



  Oil Set for Weekly Gain as Geopolitical Risks Rise 



  0805 GMT - Oil prices are on track for a significant weekly gain following news that Russia plans to temporarily ban diesel exports from resellers and extend its gasoline-export ban amid Ukrainian drone strikes against refineries. In early trade, Brent crude ticks 0.1% higher to $68.66 a barrel, while WTI is up 0.2% to $65.13 a barrel. The benchmarks continue to trade in a narrow range, but are up 3% and 4.4% on the week, respectively. Geopolitical tensions are high, with European diplomats warning the Kremlin that NATO is prepared to take action against further violations of its airspace, including by shooting down Russian planes, according to a Bloomberg report. However, further gains are capped by uncertainties over the macroeconomic outlook, with President Trump saying he would slap new tariffs on pharma and large trucks, and a looming global supply surplus. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com) 



  -- 



  Gold Holds Ground as Investors Seek Safe Haven -- Market Talk 



  0743 GMT - Gold prices tick higher in early trade, as President Trump's latest tariffs and elevated geopolitical risks due to tensions over Russian incursions into NATO airspace keep safe-haven demand strong. Futures are up 0.1% to $3,775.80 a troy ounce and on track for a weekly gain of 1.9%, while the U.S. dollar index is down 0.1% to 98.40. The precious metal is also supported by strong ETF inflows, with holdings at their highest since 2022. Investors now await a key U.S. inflation print due later on Friday for more cues into the Federal Reserve's interest-rate path this year. Meanwhile, the latest U.S. data showed that weekly jobless claims fell, while the economy grew faster than previously estimated, easing concerns about the job market. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com) 



  -- 



  China's 2035 Goal for Solar, Wind Power Capacity Seen as Conservative -- Market Talk 



  0732 GMT - China's new 2035 target of 3,600GW of solar and wind power capacity looks conservative and will likely be exceeded, Deutsche Bank analyst Gary Zhou says. He expects supply-side reform to remain the near-term focus, as restoring supply-demand balance in the renewable sector is key for investors. On the demand side, Zhou forecasts China will add 1,455GW of solar and 600GW of wind power capacities during the 2026-30 period, well above additions in 2021-25. DB's top renewables pick is GCL Tech, citing its low valuation and potential gains from solar supply reforms. Shares last traded at HK$1.27. (jason.chau@wsj.com) 



  -- 



  Comex Gold Futures' Consolidation Phase in Play, Chart Shows -- Market Talk 



  (MORE TO FOLLOW) Dow Jones Newswires



  September 26, 2025 09:06 ET (13:06 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: DHA, Housing Solutions for North Texas, Secures $69 Million Bond Reservation to Redevelop Roseland Affordable Housing Communities in Dallas
data.description:
DHA, Housing Solutions for North Texas, Secures $69 Million Bond Reservation to Redevelop Roseland Affordable Housing Communities in Dallas

The redevelopment of Roseland Townhomes and Roseland Estates will provide 274 modernized affordable housing options

Following the approval of the $69 million bond reservation, DHA, Housing Solutions for North Texas (“DHA”), is moving forward with development activities for the historic affordable housing properties located in Central Dallas, the Roseland Townhomes and Roseland Estates. With a total reservation of $69,152,737 approved by the Texas Bond Review Board (BRB), DHA has secured a crucial financing milestone, positioning the agency to advance renovations across 274 units to address local affordable housing needs and preserve the established community.

“Innovative financing is key to our ability to maximize available resources while minimizing the financial impact on taxpayers, all while delivering sustainable, high-quality housing that preserves long-term affordability for Central Dallas residents,” said Troy Brossard, president and chief executive officer of DHA. “With the BRB’s approval, we now have the means to secure critical financing for the project. DHA will continue to explore additional resources to maximize impact and ensure long-term benefits for the community.”

DHA Board Chair Betty Culbreath, an affordable housing advocate and longtime community leader, emphasized the importance of strategic investment in meeting local needs. “The approval of this bond reservation is critical to our ability to strategically enlist and deploy the resources necessary to responsibly invest in this historic community. It lays the initial groundwork necessary for preserving affordability, strengthening connections, and creating opportunities that will benefit residents and the broader neighborhood in the years ahead,” Culbreath said.

The Roseland Communities are set to undergo a comprehensive renovation to enhance living conditions and community amenities. Scheduled for completion in 2027, the project will modernize units with new kitchens, bathrooms, plumbing, flooring, appliances, and fixtures, along with extensive exterior upgrades. Improvements will also include upgraded building infrastructure, enhanced security through secured vehicular access gates at each entry point, and significant landscaping and recreation center enhancements, all designed to benefit residents and the broader Dallas community.

About DHA, Housing Solutions for North Texas

(a/k/a The Housing Authority of the City of Dallas, Texas or DHA)

DHA, Housing Solutions for North Texas, provides quality, affordable housing to low-income families and individuals through the effective and efficient administration of housing assistance programs. The Agency aims to create opportunities for program participants to achieve self-sufficiency and economic independence. DHA provides housing opportunities to ~55,700 people through public housing developments and Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) programs. DHA’s mission is to provide affordable, quality housing and access to supportive resources across North Texas. The agency is governed by its Board of Commissioners and administers housing programs funded and regulated by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development. DHA is an independent, local government entity not affiliated with the City of Dallas Housing/Community Services department, which is governed by the City of Dallas. For more information about DHA, please visit www.dhantx.com.

Media Contact:
Jacqueline Chen
CONNECTIVE Agency
start@connectiveagency.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250926676700/en/

data.title: AMGEN ANNOUNCES $650M EXPANSION OF U.S. MANUFACTURING, CREATING HUNDREDS OF NEW JOBS
data.description:
AMGEN ANNOUNCES $650M EXPANSION OF U.S. MANUFACTURING, CREATING HUNDREDS OF NEW JOBS

AMGEN ANNOUNCES $650M EXPANSION OF U.S. MANUFACTURING, CREATING HUNDREDS OF NEW JOBS

PR Newswire

THOUSAND OAKS, Calif., Sept. 26, 2025

THOUSAND OAKS, Calif., Sept. 26, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Amgen (NASDAQ: AMGN) today announced a $650 million expansion of its U.S. manufacturing network, creating hundreds of new jobs.

The planned investment will support increased drug production at the company's biologics manufacturing facility in Juncos and integrate innovative advanced technologies throughout the operations process. It is expected to create nearly 750 jobs, including construction roles and new highly skilled manufacturing jobs. 

"This expansion underscores Amgen's commitment to U.S. biomanufacturing and to strengthening the resilience of our global supply chain," said Robert A. Bradway, chairman and chief executive officer at Amgen. "By growing our capacity to deliver innovative medicines with cutting edge technology in our manufacturing plants, we will not only better serve patients but also create high-quality jobs that reinforce America's leadership in biotechnology."

"Amgen's multimillion-dollar expansion reflects a bond of mutual loyalty: Amgen's sustained investment and Puerto Rico's skilled, resilient workforce reinforcing each other over time," said Puerto Rico Governor Jenniffer González Colón. "This new expansion not only strengthens U.S. supply chain security through reshoring, it also opens new horizons for our people as we build an innovation ecosystem that positions Puerto Rico as the strongest biopharmaceutical hub in the United States."

"Puerto Rico boasts more than 60 years of expertise and excellence in the biopharmaceutical sector, supported by highly skilled talent and world-class infrastructure," said Sebastián Negrón Reichard, Puerto Rico Secretary of the Department of Economic Development and Commerce. "This investment by Amgen is a testament to how committed we are to advancing a competitive and innovative ecosystem that allows global companies to grow and prosper in Puerto Rico."

Amgen's long-standing commitment to U.S. innovation and state-of-the-art operations is reflected in more than $40 billion invested in manufacturing and research and development since the passage of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) of 2017. The enactment of pro-growth tax policies in TCJA, extended and reinforced by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025, further facilitates Amgen's ability to invest domestically in cutting-edge science and manufacturing.

This announcement builds on Amgen's recent investments, including a $600 million science and innovation center in California and manufacturing expansions of $900 million in Ohio and $1 billion in North Carolina, respectively.

About Amgen 
Amgen discovers, develops, manufactures and delivers innovative medicines to help millions of patients in their fight against some of the world's toughest diseases. More than 40 years ago, Amgen helped to establish the biotechnology industry and remains on the cutting-edge of innovation, using technology and human genetic data to push beyond what's known today. Amgen is advancing a broad and deep pipeline that builds on its existing portfolio of medicines to treat cancer, heart disease, osteoporosis, inflammatory diseases and rare diseases.

In 2024, Amgen was named one of the "World's Most Innovative Companies" by Fast Company and one of "America's Best Large Employers" by Forbes, among other external recognitions. Amgen is one of the 30 companies that comprise the Dow Jones Industrial Average®, and it is also part of the Nasdaq-100 Index®, which includes the largest and most innovative non-financial companies listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market based on market capitalization. 

For more information, visit Amgen.com and follow Amgen on X, LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube and Threads.

Amgen Forward-Looking Statements
This news release contains forward-looking statements that are based on the current expectations and beliefs of Amgen. All statements, other than statements of historical fact, are statements that could be deemed forward-looking statements, including any statements on the outcome, benefits and synergies of collaborations, or potential collaborations, with any other company (including BeOne Medicines Ltd. or Kyowa Kirin Co., Ltd.), the performance of Otezla® (apremilast), our acquisitions of ChemoCentryx, Inc. or Horizon Therapeutics plc (including the prospective performance and outlook of Horizon's business, performance and opportunities, and any potential strategic benefits, synergies or opportunities expected as a result of such acquisition), as well as estimates of revenues, operating margins, capital expenditures, cash, other financial metrics, expected legal, arbitration, political, regulatory or clinical results or practices, customer and prescriber patterns or practices, reimbursement activities and outcomes, effects of pandemics or other widespread health problems on our business, outcomes, progress, and other such estimates and results. Forward-looking statements involve significant risks and uncertainties, including those discussed below and more fully described in the Securities and Exchange Commission reports filed by Amgen, including our most recent annual report on Form 10-K and any subsequent periodic reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K. Unless otherwise noted, Amgen is providing this information as of the date of this news release does not undertake any obligation to update any forward-looking statements contained in this document as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

No forward-looking statement can be guaranteed and actual results may differ materially from those we project. Our results may be affected by our ability to successfully market both new and existing products domestically and internationally, clinical and regulatory developments involving current and future products, sales growth of recently launched products, competition from other products including biosimilars, difficulties or delays in manufacturing our products and global economic conditions, including those resulting from geopolitical relations and government actions. In addition, sales of our products are affected by pricing pressure, political and public scrutiny and reimbursement policies imposed by third-party payers, including governments, private insurance plans and managed care providers and may be affected by regulatory, clinical and guideline developments and domestic and international trends toward managed care and healthcare cost containment. Furthermore, our research, testing, pricing, marketing and other operations are subject to extensive regulation by domestic and foreign government regulatory authorities. We or others could identify safety, side effects or manufacturing problems with our products, including our devices, after they are on the market. Our business may be impacted by government investigations, litigation and product liability claims. In addition, our business may be impacted by the adoption of new tax legislation or exposure to additional tax liabilities. Further, while we routinely obtain patents for our products and technology, the protection offered by our patents and patent applications may be challenged, invalidated or circumvented by our competitors, or we may fail to prevail in present and future intellectual property litigation. We perform a substantial amount of our commercial manufacturing activities at a few key facilities, including in Puerto Rico, and also depend on third parties for a portion of our manufacturing activities, and limits on supply may constrain sales of certain of our current products and product candidate development. An outbreak of disease or similar public health threat, and the public and governmental effort to mitigate against the spread of such disease, could have a significant adverse effect on the supply of materials for our manufacturing activities, the distribution of our products, the commercialization of our product candidates, and our clinical trial operations, and any such events may have a material adverse effect on our product development, product sales, business and results of operations. We rely on collaborations with third parties for the development of some of our product candidates and for the commercialization and sales of some of our commercial products. In addition, we compete with other companies with respect to many of our marketed products as well as for the discovery and development of new products. Discovery or identification of new product candidates or development of new indications for existing products cannot be guaranteed and movement from concept to product is uncertain; consequently, there can be no guarantee that any particular product candidate or development of a new indication for an existing product will be successful and become a commercial product. Further, some raw materials, medical devices and component parts for our products are supplied by sole third-party suppliers. Certain of our distributors, customers and payers have substantial purchasing leverage in their dealings with us. The discovery of significant problems with a product similar to one of our products that implicate an entire class of products could have a material adverse effect on sales of the affected products and on our business and results of operations. Our efforts to collaborate with or acquire other companies, products or technology, and to integrate the operations of companies or to support the products or technology we have acquired, may not be successful. There can be no guarantee that we will be able to realize any of the strategic benefits, synergies or opportunities arising from the Horizon acquisition, and such benefits, synergies or opportunities may take longer to realize than expected. We may not be able to successfully integrate Horizon, and such integration may take longer, be more difficult or cost more than expected. A breakdown, cyberattack or information security breach of our information technology systems could compromise the confidentiality, integrity and availability of our systems and our data. Our stock price is volatile and may be affected by a number of events. Our business and operations may be negatively affected by the failure, or perceived failure, of achieving our sustainability objectives. The effects of global climate change and related natural disasters could negatively affect our business and operations. Global economic conditions may magnify certain risks that affect our business. Our business performance could affect or limit the ability of our Board of Directors to declare a dividend or our ability to pay a dividend or repurchase our common stock. We may not be able to access the capital and credit markets on terms that are favorable to us, or at all.

data.title: Octagos Named Among TIME's World's Top HealthTech Companies 2025
data.description:
Octagos Named Among TIME's World's Top HealthTech Companies 2025

Octagos Named Among TIME's World's Top HealthTech Companies 2025

PR Newswire

HOUSTON, Sept. 26, 2025

Exclusive Recognition Highlights Octagos as the First Choice in Remote Cardiac Monitoring

HOUSTON, Sept. 26, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Octagos, the leader in AI-enabled, vendor-agnostic cardiac remote monitoring, has been named one of the World's Top HealthTech Companies 2025 by TIME. This inaugural ranking highlights 400 companies worldwide, with Octagos earning a place among just 82 companies recognized in the highly competitive AI & Data Analytics category – and standing alone as the only remote cardiac monitoring company to earn recognition.

Octagos earned recognition due to performance in three categories:

  1. Financial: stability, growth potential, and operational efficiency.  
  2. Reputation: public perception, sentiment, visibility, and credibility across news coverage, blogs, forums, and social media activity. 
  3. Engagement: reach and engagement levels achieved through digital platforms.

Founded in Houston in 2020 by an electrophysiologist, Octagos has rapidly evolved from an innovative startup into the first choice for clinics, hospitals, and health systems modernizing their cardiac monitoring programs. Backed by investment from Morgan Stanley Expansion Capital and selected for Microsoft's exclusive Pegasus Program, Octagos has been recognized as a growth-stage healthcare company with proven scale and impact. These external endorsements, now reinforced by inclusion in TIME's global ranking, highlight Octagos' position as a technology-forward partner trusted by both industry leaders and independent evaluators.

"TIME's recognition reflects what our customers experience every day: Octagos brings clarity, accuracy, and speed to cardiac care," said Dr. Shanti Bansal, Founder and CEO of Octagos. "Many who have switched from legacy systems or competitors tell us they have faced long implementations, hidden fees, backlogged reports, and even limits on accessing their own patient data.  With Octagos, the experience is fast, transparent, and reliable. To the hospitals and health systems that already trust us, thank you. And to those considering a switch, this recognition is a clear signal that you can feel confident in choosing Octagos."

Most Octagos implementations are completed in less than four weeks, and clinics retain access to their full historical patient data, often more than ten years, when migrating from legacy platforms. The company's Human-in-the-Loop AI approach, which combines accredited US-based cardiac device experts and proprietary Atlas AI™, has been validated in peer-reviewed research, showing reductions in alert burden while increasing accuracy of clinical interpretations. Octagos technology rapidly integrates with electronic health records, using SMART on FHIR standards and single sign-on protocols to ensure secure, efficient workflows for hospitals and health systems. Octagos continues to innovate with app-based technology, offering  OctaLink, a clinical companion app for mobile access to the Octagos platform on iOS and Android, and OctaCare, a patient-facing app in pre-commercial release.

"Being selected by TIME is especially meaningful because the evaluation spanned financial performance, industry reputation, and online engagement," said Eric Olsen, Chief Operating Officer of Octagos. "Excelling across all three demonstrates the strength of our business and the trust we've earned in the market. For our customers, it reinforces confidence that they are aligned with the leader in remote cardiac monitoring. And for those exploring their options, it offers independent confirmation that Octagos has the scale, stability, and innovation to support them - not just today, but for years to come."

Octagos' selection to TIME's World's Top HealthTech Companies 2025 confirms its role as a trusted partner in cardiac remote monitoring. Recognized for strength, reputation, and engagement, and supported by Microsoft's Pegasus Program and Morgan Stanley Expansion Capital, Octagos offers hospitals and health systems a partner they can depend on as they modernize cardiac care.

About Octagos

Octagos is redefining remote cardiac monitoring with AI-powered automation, seamless EHR integrations, and industry-leading accuracy. Our advanced Atlas AI technology delivers the most precise clinical reports, significantly reducing workload while enhancing patient care. With the fastest bi-directional integrations in the industry, clinics gain real-time access to critical data without extra costs or delays. Octagos' automation eliminates non-billable noise, streamlines workflows, and ensures every alert matters. Backed by cost-effective, contract-flexible solutions and secure data migration, we empower clinics to operate efficiently without compromise. Learn more at www.octagos.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/octagos-named-among-times-worlds-top-healthtech-companies-2025-302567812.html

SOURCE Octagos Health, Inc.

data.title: enVVeno Medical (NASDAQ:NVNO) Participates in Virtual Investor "What This Means" Segment
data.description:
Watch the "What This Means" video here

IRVINE, CA / ACCESS Newswire / September 26, 2025 / enVVeno Medical Corporation (NASDAQ:NVNO) ("enVVeno" or the "Company"), a company setting new standards of care for the treatment of deep venous disease, today announced that Robert Berman, Chief Executive Officer of enVVeno Medical participated in a Virtual Investor "What This Means" segment.

As part of the segment, Mr. Berman discussed the Company's recent news announcing it filed a request for supervisory appeal of the not-approvable letter from the Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH) of the U.S. Food & Drug Administration (FDA) received on August 19, 2025, in response to its Premarket Approval (PMA) application for the VenoValve®, a surgical replacement venous valve for treating severe deep chronic venous insufficiency (CVI).

The Virtual Investor "What This Means" segment featuring enVVeno Medical is now available here.

About enVVeno Medical Corporation

enVVeno Medical (NASDAQ:NVNO) is an Irvine, California-based, late clinical-stage medical device Company focused on the advancement of innovative bioprosthetic (tissue-based) solutions to improve the standard of care for the treatment of deep venous disease. The Company's lead product, the VenoValve®, is a first-in-class surgical replacement venous valve being developed for the treatment of severe deep Chronic Venous Insufficiency (CVI). The Company is also developing a non-surgical, transcatheter based replacement venous valve for the treatment of deep venous CVI called enVVe®. Both the VenoValve and enVVe are designed to act as one-way valves, to help assist in propelling blood up the leg, and back to the heart and lungs. The Company is currently performing the final testing necessary to seek IDE approval from the FDA to begin the U.S. pivotal trial for enVVe.

data.title: Canada GDP Rises 0.2% in July
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart 




  OTTAWA--The Canadian economy grew more strongly than expected in July, buoyed by a recovery in goods-producing industries and an expansion in mining and oil and gas extraction. 



  Gross domestic product, a broad measure of goods and services produced across the economy, increased 0.2% from the month before to 2.292 trillion Canadian dollars, the equivalent of $1.644 trillion, Statistics Canada said Friday. 



  Economists were expecting GDP to have edged up 0.1%, in line with the data agency's earlier flash estimate. It follows 0.1% contractions in industry-level activity each month of the second quarter. 



  On a one-year basis, GDP expanded 0.9%. 



  The data agency said that advance information indicates GDP was essentially unchanged in August, though the estimate will be updated. 





  Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  September 26, 2025 08:49 ET (12:49 GMT)
